Why Enterprise Security Standards Feel So Confusing
If you’ve worked on enterprise software, cloud platforms, fintech, healthcare, or government systems, you’ve run into the same wall of acronyms: ISO 27001, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect (OIDC), OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASVS, TLS, PCI DSS.
Read in isolation, they look unrelated. Some are compliance frameworks. Others are authentication protocols. Some define secure coding practices. Others govern payment security or encrypted transport. It’s natural to ask the question underneath all of this:
How do these standards actually fit together in a real architecture?
The short answer: they don’t compete with each other. They protect different layers of the same system, and none of them is sufficient on its own.
This guide breaks down each standard from first principles, shows how they compose into a working enterprise architecture, and covers how organizations in the European Union (EU) and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) layer regional regulation on top of them.
